bounded_scope: These parity cases compare arc-solvers interactive runner artifacts across the retained local offline wrapper and the local compatibility-server-backed remote wrapper. They cover action execution parity, checkpoint resume, recording parity, and scorecard closeout behavior on one bounded fixture. Competition mode remains an explicit remote-only policy surface and is documented here as a non-parity divergence instead of being hidden as a mismatch.

documented_expected_differences.reason: local runs use the offline wrapper and remote runs use the compatibility-server-backed remote wrapper
the interactive run report preserves offline for local runs and online for remote runs
recording envelopes preserve the environment operation mode rather than normalizing it away
local scorecard ids are runner-owned fixture ids while remote ids come from the scorecard service
local and remote sessions mint independent environment guids
local environments retain the fixture package path while remote listings omit local filesystem details
only the remote scorecard surface materializes a closeout summary
recording digests differ because operation mode is part of the recording contract
checkpoint metadata preserves the environment-specific recording digest

extra_expected_differences.reason: competition mode is documented as a deliberate non-parity path until authoritative online semantics exist
